summary: Cleaned up and renamed menu/game-mode data structures and fields, with matching work centered on mnmain and a new/filled mnruleplus implementation. The PR replaced many anonymous or offset-style names with semantic names for menu scene enter/exit data, tournament/menu option state, controller repeat state, PAD virtual direction/action masks, and unlock feature flags. It also moved menu-related callback payloads into typed structs and added forward declarations/static size assertions where evidence was available.
what_changed: Large menu cleanup touched 31 files with the largest churn in src/melee/mn/mnmain.c and a new/filled src/melee/mn/mnruleplus.c.

Added Dolphin PAD virtual input constants beyond raw button bits, including PAD_STICK_* directions, PAD_SUBSTICK_* directions, PAD_TRIGGER_LR, PAD_CONFIRM, PAD_CANCEL, PAD_LRAST, and PAD_ANY_* masks using 64-bit values where needed.
Renamed MinorSceneInfo payload fields from unk_struct_0 and unk_struct_1 to load_data and leave_data, with comments tying them to OnLoad and OnLeave callback data.
Added gm forward declarations for MenuExitData and MenuEnterData and defined those structs in gm/types.h with 0x4-byte STATIC_ASSERTs.
Renamed TmData fields from offset names to semantic names such as cur_option, match_type, combatants, entrants, hmn_cpu_count, stage_selection_type, and cpu_level.

Updated users of TmData fields across game-mode files, for example gm_18A5.c, gm_1B03.c, and gmtou.c now reference cur_option, entrants, match_type, and stage_selection_type instead of x0, xC, x4, and x14.
Renamed controller-map repeat fields in gm_1A36.static.h and gm_1A36.c: unk became repeat2 and x28 became repeat_timer.
Changed gm_16F1.h prototype for fn_8017280C from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void fn_8017280C(void), reducing unknown signature usage.
Renamed gmm_x1868 feature bitmask field from unk_4 to x186C and documented it as an unlocked-features bitmask while retaining uncertainty about the adjacent padding.
Added brief documentation for gm_801A4B90 and gm_801A4B9C as accessors for current scene enter and exit data.
Adjusted gmscdata.c to cast mn_8022DDA8_OnEnter to void* in a MinorSceneHandler initializer, likely to accommodate callback/payload typing without broader signature churn.
smart_moves: Renaming callback payload fields to load_data and leave_data used actual callback flow as evidence: OnLoad receives load_data and OnLeave receives leave_data in gm_1A3F.c.
MenuEnterData and MenuExitData were small typed structs with explicit field comments and STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(...) == 0x4), preserving ABI/layout confidence while improving readability.
The PAD additions preserved existing raw button names while adding higher-level virtual/action masks, making menu input logic easier to express without hiding the original bit positions.

Controller repeat-state names were derived from behavior in gm_1A36.c: trigger/release initialize repeat2 and repeat_timer, the timer decrements, and held buttons repopulate repeat2 after repeat thresholds.
Field renames were propagated across callers in gm and mn code instead of only changing struct definitions, reducing the chance of mixed old/new terminology.
Uncertain discoveries were documented conservatively, for example trigger has a 'maybe rename?' note and gmm_x1868 padding comments mention plausible but unconfirmed feature-removal history.
decomp_lessons: When a MinorScene field is only known through lifecycle callbacks, name it after the callback role first: load_data for OnLoad payloads and leave_data for OnLeave payloads.
Use static size assertions when replacing anonymous callback blobs with named structs, especially for scene-transition data shared across menu/game-mode code.

Offset names in central structs can often be replaced by examining repeated control-flow patterns across multiple files: TmData.x0 became cur_option because loops, UI selection ranges, and scene decisions consistently treated it as the current option.
Input repeat code is a good source of semantic field names: timers, held buttons, triggers, releases, and repeat masks can be distinguished by update order rather than by type alone.
Prefer conservative names when full semantics are not proven. This PR improved unk_4 to x186C with a feature-bitmask comment but did not overclaim every bit's meaning.
Header cleanup and forward declarations matter for menu decomp because menu payload structs are shared between gm scene tables, mn code, and callback accessors.
naming_conventions: Scene callback payload fields: load_data and leave_data, replacing unk_struct_0 and unk_struct_1.
Menu scene data structs: MenuEnterData for OnLoad/MJ_MENU enter payloads and MenuExitData for OnLeave/MJ_MENU exit payloads.
Menu enter fields use direct behavior names where observed: menu_kind, hovered_selection, and load_assets.
Menu exit fields use pending_major and pending_minor for next-scene targets.
Tournament/menu data fields use semantic snake_case names: cur_option, match_type, combatants, entrants, hmn_cpu_count, stage_selection_type, cpu_level.
Controller repeat fields use repeat-oriented names: repeat2 and repeat_timer.
PAD virtual masks follow existing SDK-style uppercase macro naming: PAD_STICK_UP, PAD_SUBSTICK_LEFT, PAD_TRIGGER_LR, PAD_CONFIRM, PAD_CANCEL, PAD_ANY_RIGHT.
For partially understood persistent data, this PR retained offset-derived names such as x186C while adding explanatory comments rather than inventing a definitive name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The PR body explicitly says 'mnmain matches and work' and 'mnruleplus matches and work', but the provided excerpt does not include specific assembly tricks for those matches.
Prototype narrowing from UNK_RET fn_8017280C(UNK_PARAMS) to void fn_8017280C(void) is a common matching cleanup once callsites/signature evidence is sufficient.
Using exact struct sizes for MenuEnterData and MenuExitData preserves binary layout while replacing untyped void payloads with named fields.

The gmscdata.c cast of mn_8022DDA8_OnEnter to void* suggests a pragmatic matching/typing tactic: keep table layout or handler type stable while a callback's real type differs from the generic initializer type.
64-bit PAD masks use 1ULL for bits above 31, avoiding truncation when representing menu virtual inputs as u64 values.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale was available; conclusions are based on the title, PR body excerpt, changed-file list, and diff excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Inspect full mnmain.c diff from PR #2060 to catalog exact function-level menu renames and matching fixes not visible in the excerpt.
Inspect mnruleplus.c and mnruleplus.h from PR #2060 to determine what functions or structs became matching and reusable for rule-plus menu work.
Search later PRs for MenuEnterData/MenuExitData to see whether fields x3 or padding were renamed with stronger evidence.
Search later PRs for gm_controller_map repeat2 to see whether repeat2 received a better semantic name.
Search later PRs for gmm_x1868.x186C to identify individual unlocked feature bits.
